steps.title: Fastest path: graph the equations as written
steps.content:
Type the equations into Desmos exactly as they appear, one per row:
$$\frac{2}{5}x + \frac{1}{4}y = \frac{3}{8}$$
$$ax + y = c$$
You do not need to solve or rearrange them first.

steps.title: Read the number of intersections
steps.content: For infinitely many intersections, the two graphs must be the same line. Multiplying the first equation by 4 gives $\frac{8}{5}x + y = \frac{3}{2}$, so the matching value is D, $\frac{8}{5}$.

steps.title: Why this works
steps.content: A solution to a system is exactly a point that lies on every graph in the system. When the graphs are the same line, every point on that line is shared, which is the infinite-solution case.
